name: Art Farmer
abstract: Arthur Stewart Farmer (August 21, 1928 – October 4, 1999) was an American jazz trumpeter and flugelhorn player. He also played flumpet, a trumpet–flugelhorn combination especially designed for him.

interviews.title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

concerts.label: Benefit Concert for C.O.R.E. and Virginia - Jamaica Student Help Project
JVC Jazz Festival: Re-Birth of the Cool

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Mabern
about_quotes.evidence: So I said, 'Cedar I’ve been with my wife almost fifty years, maybe because of you.' ... I was with the band, we did two records together with the band and I did one beautiful quartet date with Art Farmer called 'Perception' with Roy McCurdy, Tommy Williams and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: Art was in town and put together a band to go into the Half-Note... Art called me, said he had this gig at the Half Note for two weeks... I'm working for Art Farmer the next two weeks.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Milt Hinton
about_quotes.evidence: That was on a date with Barry Galbraith on guitar, Art Farmer on trumpet, Cannonball Adderley on saxophone, and yours truly on bass.
about_quotes.doc_title: Milt Hinton interviewed by Joe Williams, Majesty of the Seas, May 31, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Tom McIntosh
about_quotes.evidence: we haven't talked about your days with Benny Golson and Art Farmer, the jazztet. ... So, I was in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom McIntosh — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Annie Ross
about_quotes.evidence: In this band – it was Hamp’s band... the trumpet section was Quincy [Jones], Art Farmer, Clifford Brown.
about_quotes.doc_title: Annie Ross — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John LaPorta
about_quotes.evidence: And it was a combination of a jazz quintet and Art Farmer was in it, myself, and symphony orchestra.
about_quotes.doc_title: John LaPorta interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 13, 1996
